analysis.name: Karim Asghari
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 215 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: The material is overall simple but his tests are brutal. His lectures seem okay to comprehend and his study guide is helpful. The test questions are tricky despite it being multiple choice because each choice is not straight forward and has a multitude of options such as all of the above , none of the above, A and B or A,B and C or B and E.

Worst professor ever. He knows his stuff but is not a great teacher. Lectures are hard to understand. UF really needs to make this course and in-seat class. The test are very difficult to study for because his questions are very specific. He ends class 2 weeks early? Why not distribute that time throughout the course. Step it up UF!

The material itself wasn't too bad but his tests were ridiculously difficult. They're multiple choice but the options go like A, A &B, B &C, all of the above, two of the above, none of the above! You have to know the material in detail. Smokin notes help as long as you're ready to remember each and every word of it. You have to work really hard!!

This class was honestly awful. I dont understand how people thought this class was good. The exams have so many grammar mistakes and are very tricky. Buy smokin notes and literally know every little detail. do the extra credit As well. Didnt feel like the lectures were good for exams and hes not a good communicator or very nice but JMO.

This class was harder than I expected. You don't go to class, you just watch his lectures (about 3 a week, 1 hour ish each). There are 4 exams that make up your grade, but he offers some extra credit. I put this class on a pedestal and did everything in my power to do well, and I still got Cs on his exams, even when I felt confident.

analysis.name: Alex York
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 103 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This was a great class! The lectures are kind of boring and the homework takes a long time, but I think this class works really well being online. Spend maybe 2 or 3 hours a day (including the 1hr lecture) and you should be fine. Exams are very fair and straightforward, but be weary for tricks!

Busywork, busywork, busywork!! Next to zero extra credit, exams are weighted heavily. Homework is incredibly time-consuming. His Summer C lectures were disorganized and sometimes quite long. Expect a fairly straightforward but time-consuming class. Exam content is straightforward, but this class feels more like applied precalculus than calculus.

Bang average professor, long boring lectures he zips through without much explanation half the time. Homework takes a ludicrous amount of time to do for what the content is. That being said, the class concepts are not hard. Just be ready to spend 3 hours on one unit between the lecture/lecture quiz/hws.

The lecture videos were good means of learning, but they were monotone and boring especially with the long lectures at the end of the course. The exams are weighted heavily, but the exams are easy if you take the time to study and do practice exams that are provided.

There are about 2-3 lectures per week, and each lecture has a 10-20 question homework as well as a quiz with 3-5 questions. The tests are exactly like the quiz questions and are pretty easy. I never had to interact with the prof during the course because it was online and I had no technical difficulties. The beginning of course is easy, end is meh.

analysis.name: Ana Longo
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 112 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Don't be scared off by the negative reviews following the 2021 spring exam 3. Dr. Longo had detailed lectures, lots of reading (as did the other three profs) and a difficult test but overall not too bad if you studied the learning objectives. Ppl retaliated bc they got a bad grade, doesn't reflect the prof. If u can't handle bio1, hope ur not STEM.

I think many reviews here are unfair to Dr. Longo as people posted them after being upset about the exam. I found the exam to be very fair and lecture-based. I watched lectures on 2x speed the day before, studied 5 days in advance, and did very well. Dr. Longo is available in office hours which I recommend if confused, and is overall a good person.

People are being very harsh to her as a direct result of the Spring 2021 exam. I thought her lectures were really interesting and her teaching style was easy to follow. That being said, the exam was poorly worded and when the TAs predicted a 10 point curve, we got nothing. She does not care about students' success, but hey, welcome to college.

I honestly enjoyed learning from Longo. Her lectures were to the point and I liked the small jokes and memes she included. However; her exam was oddly specific and the wording was confusing; it did not reflect what we had learned that well. If you study you should be fine, but if you don't pay attention to detail you won't do well with her.

There are many other reviews on this page that are not fair to Dr. Longo. Students flooded Rate My Professor after failing the exam, which is more a way for them to express their anger than honestly evaluating the professor. Dr. Longo taught evolution very well, there werent too many assignments or lectures, and it is totally feasible to get an A.

analysis.name: Aditi Mukherjee
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 67 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: One of the easiest classes I've taken. Study early for the Excel Certification exam and attend every class. The in-class projects make up a big part of your grade. She's a good professor, but be on time for class because if you are more than 5 minutes late, she won't let you do the project. No extensions, so don't turn things in late.

Easiest critical tracking class you will ever take. Meets once a week for a project you do with a partner. If you're stuck she'll help you through it. No exams, only play posits and the excel certification/final exam. Definitely chose the excel certification its super easy. Please take this and not CGS trust me.

Most of the class is assignments with unlimited attempts and step by step instructions. Only somewhat difficult thing is the excel certification midterm but that's relatively easy too. Professor mostly just lets you learn through the online textbook and doesn't really teach herself, but its easy to get an A.

Very light class, hardest part was the Excel Certification Exam, but that wasn't even that bad. Some of her lab instructions were confusing and there was some busy work, but overall not bad at all. Only one lab a week.
Too much work for a 2 credit class and daily quizzes, projects, and assignments.

analysis.name: Ali Shafiee
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: If you can take him, do it. I had him for 3120 (by chance) and now fir 4130. Easiest A I've had fr. The 4130 average is over an A rn and hope the exams stay the same lol. He's super nice and passionate but as a newer professor so he's still figuring some things out i think. but he's very approachable and tries to adjust so not a big deal

This is the worst professor I have had at UF. Multiple slides with incorrect math, practice problems with incorrect key answers, is unable to explain any of the concepts correctly, be ready to self teach and recognize all of his work is completely wrong. Grading is fine but don't expect to learn anything of value. He's a super nice guy though.

Nice guy overall and you can tell he actually cares and listens. The class is hard and the subject is kinda boring, and you need a lot of practice, like a LOT. Still, everyone I know got an A. There are a lot of bonus point opportunities. Go to office hours and do the practice problems if you want to do well.
Be prepared for very very boring lectures (yes worse than Epstein). Exams are very hard and homework is difficult to understand. Lots of at-home learning required. Good Luck!

analysis.name: Amanda Phalin
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 111 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: You miss one detail and you are done. She packs on tons of assignments with loads of criteria to follow to a T. As a full-time online student (w/ a full time job), I don't think she understands that just maneuvering around all her loads of crap is hard enough. She treats you like a slacker if you can't keep up with it all and it feels demeaning.

Professor Phalin teaches a completely useless course. The exams are almost entirely (80%) about memorizing obscure facts about a specific country. On top of that, she berates you with 4 assignments per week that are difficult to score well on. Overall, an out of touch professor. No one cares what the GDP growth rate is in 2020 for Canada...

Since the first day of class she's had an unsettling "Not My Problem" attitude towards her students. She's one of those people that's very 'rules-based' and unaccommodating. This class is a perfect example of UF trying to make pointless classes unnecessarily difficult. Her lectures are not worth your time, Smokin' Notes are the way to go.

Professor Phalin is a great lecturer and makes class interesting despite content sometimes being boring. Definitely utilize the cheat sheet she allows you to have on the exam. I used smokin notes to help me figure out the most important information to put on it and it was a life saver!

This class was pretty easy, I didn't use Smokin' Notes, just went to all the lectures and participated. You get a full page of notes, so memorization wasn't an issue. She also gives you a template for this sheet which is really helpful and makes sure you don't miss anything you should have on your cheat sheet.

analysis.name: Amie Baisley
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 80 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: You gonna learn nothing, trust! She knows what she is teaching, for sure, but she doesn't know how to teach nor give a clear and constructive feedback. She is accessible, but yet, her explanation is just her repeating herself from the 30 min lectures that she makes us watch. If you wanna learn something, not just copying stuff, don't take her class

Organized. Good, but full Office Hours. Unreasonable expectations. Expects you to know Matlab+No grades until the end of the year. You aren't going to be able to spend the 25 hours a week to finish the HW (not hard, but statics must be done on PowerPoint) + 10-70 hrs for each coding project (once a month) with 8+ page report (with proofs + words).

All the comments with Quality 1.0 and Difficulty 5.0 are accurate. So let me persuade you to not take this class. With this class, you will contemplate dropping out of college and you will get burnt out. The lectures you watch will be filled with jargon and the homeworks and projects will make you cry. Please don't take this class lol

Nice lady but don't take her. You don't get any useful instruction and are required to learn the topics by reading through 3 worked out problems per module. Unfortunately the worked out problems aren't well annotated + some calculation steps are skipped. Also, her method is very odd so you cant use outside resources to cross-reference the content.

Flipped classroom, except the material you're expected to review before helps little with the problems that you're expected to solve in class. Attendance is mandatory for homework sessions that you don't really get anything out of, and the grading system requires you to do everything her way or you'll lose credit despite a right answer.

analysis.name: Stephen Adams
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 134 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Every type of problem that could show up on a test is in his notes. He's very detailed and goes at a good pace. Lectures are recorded, homework is tedious (as with all math) but incredibly doable with unlimited attempts. I like math, but even if you don't I think he makes the subject accessible and comprehensible to anyone.

He's a good lecturer for most people but I couldn't grasp the content and struggled. Adams & Calc 2 just isn't for me I guess. The exams are brutal. Study ahead of time by using the past exams and resources on canvas. Don't forget to do homework or particpation quizzes. The TAs are helpful. Set up 1 on 1 meetings with them if you are struggling.

Amazing math professor that made Calculus 2 bearable. He has in-depth lectures that explain not only how certain techniques work but also why they work. The Xronos assignments are annoying sometimes, but understandable for this class, and even if the tests are difficult he properly prepares his students for every question within his lectures.

Calc 2 is horrendous (especially for the 2nd time since I have to retake lol) but I feel like if you genuinely keep up and practice the homework every single day, you'll do fine. It just moves extremely quick and I was always behind. Felt like the other prof (Chui) gave more shortcuts/tips on her notes/office h, I ended up going to her to learn tbh

Adams is such a kind, funny, caring individual who I love having as a professor (please be my father). He makes the material feel natural and go by quickly, and I didn't struggle in this class because of him. I highly recommend going to lectures to not fall behind, but it goes by quickly and the homework is short. 10/10 teacher, would take again.

analysis.name: Ashlyn Hale
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 114 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: From Chem 1 to Chem 2, Dr. Hale completely changed the format of the class. Very quiz and exam-heavy, so be warned. Exams are designed to "trick" you to test your knowledge. Her lectures are not super helpful and she does not do a lot of practice problems in class. She is although very bubbly and has a lot of character which makes the class upbeat.

I failed the class without even knowing till after the add/drop date. The whole semester, the grade on canvas was a lie and she expected us to calculate our own grades but it doesn't say anywhere that we had to do that. She never finishes lectures on time+If you have a sickness or cannot come to class, she makes it difficult for you to get excused.

Do NOT take Dr. Hale. There is a reason chem 2 is an 85+ is an A. Her lectures are useless and the tests are hard! She writes questions purposefully to trick students. Take it with Dr. Benjamin instead. Exams and in person quizzes are 93% of your grade, so if you mess up one you're cooked. Study edge was a lifesaver and is why I got an A-.

Not sure why she gets good reviews. Lectures were rushed and disorganized, and she spent too much singing and not enough time on lecture content. Made it impossible to focus on the actual chem and was honestly kind of rude.

You're better off just teaching yourself everything, she's inconstant with what she teaches in different lectures depending on the time of day, and generally explains almost every concept poorly. Chem 1 has relatively very easy material, but she still finds a way to explain it bad enough and jump around topics enough you'll leave lecture confused.

analysis.name: Andres Rubiano Acosta
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 101 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Lectures are "short" but so dense you pause every second if you actually want to understand the content. Excel calculators he made us create were just time wasters, since only the first exam had us using them. Hardly communicated with us and gave zero feedback. Easy exams if your work is done, but overcomplicates how he wants us to prepare for them

DO NOT take this professor. It takes at least 10 hours of work a week to earn an A, and he requires that you record your screen for all assignments. He cares about his students and is very knowledgeable, but he has no grasp on how difficult the class is for those with no CAD experience. I would only recommend him If you have an easy semester ahead.

Possibly one of the most miserable experiences I have had at UF thus far. Quizzes do not show up on the canvas assignment page, little to no feedback on submissions or exams, the grading scale is way too harsh given that the class is graded solely by 4 exams.

Worst experience I've had. The professor is disrespectful in emails and the TAs never reply. Lectures consist of old YouTube videos that only cover the basics, but the exams are incredibly advanced and feel completely disconnected from the teaching material. It feels like being taught addition and then being tested on calculus.

The exams are as fair as they can come, and your final grade in the class depends on them only. Quizzes and HW are for practice. Dr Rubiano's lectures are fast-paced and to the point. You learn everything you need in minutes. Really organized online resources and he's always available to meet over Zoom.

analysis.name: Garrett Beatty
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Best professor ever! Exams are easy, he's very flexible, and he believes in AI like many Gen Z. Highly recommended.

Great class overall and assignments were pretty easy and straight to the point. Please be advised that the Exams are hard and are heavy scenario based. You will not have enough time to ponder on any of the questions as you have very little time to complete the exam with so many scenario based questions. So be prepared for that...

Absolutely loved this professor. Such a helpful and knowledgable guy. The assignments aren't too bad and the quizzes and tests are easy. Ultimately, it's a relatively easy A. Definitely one of my favorite professors at UF thus far.

Quizzes are easy. There are only 2 exams but they are straightforward, as long as you study. You don't need the textbook as long as you watch the lectures diligently and take notes. The assignments are hard. They are a lot of work and he really does expect you to be an expert in the field. He's a tough grader. The class, ultimately, isn't too bad.

Dr. Beatty was great for exercise psych! You definitely have to perform well on both of the projects (which are graded somewhat harshly) as well as the midterm and final to get an A. He offers substantial extra credit for doing optional discussions (which are horrible, but helpful). In addition, he was super kind and flexible surrounding COVID.

analysis.name: Glen Billesbach
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Glen succeeded in making what should have been a manageable course unnecessarily difficult and frustrating. Not only did lectures lack any coherency or structure, important concepts were too often explained in a confusing way or not at all. He also rarely gave assignment instructions that were complete.

He is an amazing professor. He truly understands students, and is a great lecturer, even though it could be heavy on some days. It's a required class for Sustainability Majors, and I really enjoyed it.

I had Glen for the Fall 2023 semester for American Political Thought, and he was a very informative and kind professor who truly cared about the material he chose and the student's involvement in the course.

He's the best! If you're looking for a passionate professor with clear grading outlines, great office hours, and an overall happy demeanor: TAKE HIS CLASS. The texts are super interesting, not too difficult to understand, and he's super understanding if you have problems in class. Just listen in on the lectures and you'll do great on the essays.

Professor Billesbach is hands down one of the best university professors at UF! His lectures are fun, easy to understand, and he really tries to break down the content so each student understands the material. He encourages participation, lectures are great, and the readings are super interesting. Take this professor for any POT class at UF!

analysis.name: Leon Brown
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The class itself is challenging without a textbook and in such timeframe (8wks), but he gave us soo much material to review even without a textbook. He also makes himself available to help students through regular office hours or you can just schedule a teams meeting with him. I would say it's a lot for a 2credit class but he's a good professor.

This course has way too many assignments. There are quizzes, labs, discussions, and two exams. He covers way too much material in 6 weeks. For the quizzes and exams, I felt very overwhelmed with the amount of material I had to know. I would have to constantly study to keep up. If there is another professor, take the class with them.

I've had Dr.Brown for both ISM3254 and ISM3255 now and I think he is incredible. I've had many coding professors in the past who can't seem to grasp the fact that coding is a new subject for many students. Dr. Brown walks you through Java so clearly and the assignments all have purpose. He is a gem of a professor.

Poor course structure across all his classes. With such a bad rate my professor at his previous school it's a mystery why UF hired him. He seems almost panicked teaching and gets himself always behind which puts us at risk consistently. Doesn't look out for student success at all.
He assigns convoluted assignments and never grades them so you do not know how well youre doing in the class. It is a 2 credit class that takes like 6 hours each weeek

analysis.name: Bojan Lazarevic
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. L is the best professor! He is supportive and accessible, and he is willing to help with anything ID related. His assignment feedback is extremely personalized (and often is in video/audio clip form). I marked average difficulty, but I really mean, he is fair. I had a wonderful experience in both his courses I took. Would 100% recommend!

Dr. L is amazing. He is accessible through email and truly wants to support students. Also, his courses' designs are great. He is a fair grader and very kind. Ask him anything EdTech and he will certainly help!

As long as you submit your assignment by the deadline, you're going to be fine. No busy work. Everything is planned with a purpose. Dr. L is really good about leaving personalized video feedback and making an effort to know students. I have taken three classes with him and he is by far one of my favorite professors.

The professor is amazing and always responds within a reasonable time. He is a super fair grader and always leaves great feedback on assignments and posts. The course work is not difficult as long as you read the instructions and understand the topics you should do great.

Dr. L is a very respected professor at the University. His modules can be wordy and uninteresting at times, but he explains everything very thoroughly and assigns lots of in-class assignments for credit. If you need help with something, he is always willing to go out of his way for his students. I highly recommend any of his classes!

analysis.name: John Banko
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 357 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Probably the worst class/professor of your college career. You'll be taught how to do the work in Excel, but you can't use Excel on the exams. Why? Well, in his own words, he's "evil." His advice is to run up and down the stadium stairs then take practice exams. Very practical. I felt confident based on lectures and practice problems, failed exams.

This guy is ridiculous. Flat out refuses to discuss exam grading or what questions may have been missed, even though he reserves right to test on those types of questions again. Honestly, doesn't care about you learning or not, just that he went through the motions. If majority of students are failing a test, is that not on the teacher?

If you could have taken this class a year ago with Tapley and didn't, kick yourself, because you just made the biggest mistake of your college career. Lack of a formula sheet for 50-100 formulas, no more charts that used to be given. Unnecessarily horrible for fairly reasonable material. The free response will destroy your will to live.

Banko is the worst instructor ever and his class is just awful. I am a straight-A student, 4.0 GPA, working on honors thesis. I worked VERY HARD in this class, paid for tutoring, and sacrificed a lot of time studying, but my best was not good enough. I still ended up with a C. I am not a finance major, but good luck to those who need an A or B.

What a horrible teacher.  Took it over Summer C and was a total waste of time and money.  He doesn't teach - he talks at you.  Test questions are many times harder than examples in the book, don't even try. My favorite saying was, we have too many finance majors so anyone who would normally get an A will get a C.   FABULOUS

analysis.name: Stephanie Bogart
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 39 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I'm currently taking this class and it's being treated more as a writing class than a Quest class. Both the professor and TA are hard graders, every assignment I've submitted has had numerous comments on it, and I've very rarely gotten a 100% on anything. It's taken way too seriously for what it is. Just take a different class.

Dr. Bogart is one of my favorite professors at UF thus far. You can tell that she truly cares! Attendance is not mandatory, but she gives extra credit if you go. The exams are also a little tricky, but if you do your readings and take notes in lectures, there's no reason why you wouldn't get an A/B.

Exams were easy and open note. The lectures weren't really interesting. The writing assignments were quite doable. Just make sure to start on the last 3000-word final paper a week before it's due. The discussions were fun and you didn't have to prepare a lot for them.

I took this without the WR, so I can't speak on that. The lectures were engaging and she knows what she's talking about - I came to respect her a lot. The tests are ridiculously easy, and I barely remember the homework, so insignificant. The TA section was also a lot of fun, but my TA was a hilarious grad student who barely cared, so that helped.

Fun and SUPER EASY course. I spent minimal time on this course and easily got an A. Exams are open note, but very specific to the lectures. Watch lectures (super short) and take notes on a computer. There is 2 kinda big projects that have clear instructions and don't take too long. She offers EC zoom sessions where you can ask questions.

analysis.name: Rori Bloom
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I've had Dr Bloom for three classes now and she is interesting to say the least. She is not flexible with new ideas/different approaches to prompts, but is a very nice woman. She will eventually give you an A if you participate, but if you are used to AP Lit style writing where you go beyond the prompt, be prepared to be flamed for it at first.

While this class interesting I cannot recommend Dr. Bloom. Her grading criteria was completely random and she did not give clarification when asked. She only has like four assignments the entire semester, so one error was detrimental, I asked her for help and she refused, then failed me anyway. If you need any type of accommodations, she will hate.

I took her fairy tales class and her enthusiasm was contagious. She is somewhat of a tough grader without super clear expectations, but she does an excellent job of teaching course content. 50% of the grade is made up of 2 essays, each with 1000 words, the rest of the grade comes from discussions and participation. I would def recommend for quest 1

Interesting material and I can only say good things about this professor for her teaching. Most UF professors are easy A's, but this is not one of them.Fortunately, the curve will save you and your final grade will turn out to be good.But trust me, you'll be worried every minute until you get that final grade.I guess she likes to motivate her stude

In the beginning of the semester, I was really apprehensive about her because of some of the comments I read on ratemyprofessors, but it turned out that she's a really great professor.  I learned so much.  She's very nice and very intelligent.  Her instructions are clear, and her grading is fair.  I would definitely take another class from her. =)

analysis.name: Sylvie Blum
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: My goat. SO entertaining, really values participation and getting to know her students. Values ideas over grammar. Let me throw a backpack across the room as part of a scene (this class was 20th century french theater) and gave me extra credit for it. Love her down.
Dr. Blum is amazing! She genuinely wants to help you and is super flexible. She's funny in class too. Go to office hours just to talk to her! I wish I had taken her classes sooner!

She is  truly passionate about fashion and film. I learned so much, and it was genuinely a pleasure to attend her class. The structure was well-organized you watch the films at home and discuss them later in the week, which allows for better time management. She is also also very understanding and approachable.

Professor Blum is a fantastic professor who cares about her students. You must do the homework and be prepared to speak in class. She randomly calls on students to speak which is intimidating at first but it leads to great improvement. Just be ready to be called on 1-3 times each class. I would totally recommend her if you have the chance.

Took this my 1st sem & was worried I wouldn't keep up - she speaks French 95% of the time. Felt way more comfortable by a month in. Truly cares abt her students, gives lots of not-that-hard small assignments, generous grader. 5 "major" essays, 1-2 pages, interesting topics. Very French & endearing humor. Discussion participation matters. Love her!

analysis.name: Laura Cruz Castro
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 59 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I loved her class! She cares a lot and it shows. Her tests were not the best, they were hard and there was A LOT for relatively little time. Projects were better. She gives you a lot of time, explains the necessary things, and is flexible with deadlines when a lot of students are struggling. Very understanding and caring, skilled teacher.

This class wasn't the easiest but also wasn't the hardest. If you already know python/coding then itll be a breeze but if you're completely new it's kind of tricky if you're taking it with other hard classes. Assignments arent too bad, Labs are kind of time consuming,+theres usually a 2-q quiz weekly. Projects are DEVIOUS. Exams are ok but get you

She is so chill, if you can take this class with her, do it. Avoid the in person sections of Python with the other profs, heard they are much harder. Everything is open note and there's extra credit on almost anything. Super responsive outside of class and clear on grading. The homeworks can take a little while, but besides that easy A class.

Amazing professor. I was taking the in-person Python coding section and struggling, but her online section is very slow-paced and thorough, so I felt like it was much easier to understand the language. Very helpful and kind, responds fast to emails/discord and always on the side of the student/helping the student. Exams and projects are open note.

If you want a professor who genuinely cares about her students then look no further. I have no doubt in my mind that Professor Cruz wants what is best for her students and is patient, kind, and will work things through with you. Having a Professor like her is a rare and incredible experience that I highly recommend you take.

analysis.name: Carissa Nicholson
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 31 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: You don't actually interact with Professor Nicholson too much in this class, as the TAs do all the grading and the lectures are done by somebody else. However the work is pretty easy, online assignments on a weekly basis. Depending on your TA, they may grade harsher than others, but overall should still be an easy A if you put in the work.

She's almost not involved in this course its mostly you and your TA. The work was pretty simple throughout the semester besides the final project but that was also ok. What really bothered me was how long it took to release the grades for the museum essay. I turned it in oct 5 and the grades were released dec 2 which is an insane wait time.

This class is not necessarily hard, it's just annoying. A lot of random assignments but they aren't difficult, just a waste of time if you don't care about the subject. The only time I communicated with the professor was in a Q&A discussion post. She's fine, nothing bad/good. Everything seemed to be graded by the TA's, who take forever to grade.

Gave us online "games" to play and write essays about, very straightforward. A couple of easy worksheets. Two exams that weren't bad. You don't really need to read. Skipped the last half of semester and just reviewed the slides he posted. I would recommend attending lecture at least once a week if you plan to skip though.

amazing. attendance is not mandatory, 90 is an A, 2 exams (worth only 30% of your grade), 4 short papers with lenient graders, one of which gets dropped including zeroes for no submission, and he's funny. first professor i willingly loved going to class for, please take him. not a single bad thing i have to say i hope he wins the lottery

dr. lauteria is goated. he's so enthusiastic about the course and makes going to class enjoyable. just make sure you get a good group for group projects, but even if your group sucks you'll be fine. exams r based off of his lectures/slides. despite the class being relatively easy, i definitely learned a lot!! take him if u can

Great lecturer; kept me extremely engaged. There's 2 exams that are super easy as long as you know the vocab he gives you. Four homework assignments are playing a video game, talking about them in groups, and writing short papers about them (easy and fun). Attendance is extra credit. Take this professor.

analysis.name: Emily Hind

review_highlights.comment: I had Dr. Hind for a Spanish Lit course I took while studying abroad in a six week program. She gave way too much work for the lack of time we had and it was very difficult to keep up. Her standards were too high to meet with the quantity of work given. Her quizzes had little to do with the topic of the class and I found her to be unreasonable

Do NOT take History and Politics of Caregiving. Awful class that provides no real world or invaluable information. A lot of readings that are 30-50 pages long and you spend more time doing work for this class than classes that are required for your major. Hind is enthusiastic and likes her course but it is extremely boring and useless.

Loved this class! It was a great class to take during freshman year since there's a lot of group discussion. Prof. Hind is passionate about what she teaches and makes each class interesting.
There's an assigned reading for each lecture/class. No tests but there's 4 major essays. You'll do fine if you go to her office hours and revise with her
She's a terrible teacher. The worst professor I ever had. Avoid her if you can.

HOPE Humans Oil Energy & Plants. professor is good and extremely passionate, but assigns a lot of readings that do not relate much to the essays. you can write about anything for each topic and she will grade for credit. go to office hours and talk with her to discuss how to improve paper, will not be a hard grader. LOTS OF READINGS!

analysis.name: Emily Miller-Cushon
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Miller-Cushon is a very caring and understanding professor. She will provide you with extensions when you need it. The class is made up of a few assignments, participation, and quizzes. This class is super easy, I personally was not a fan of the lectures due to pacing-- I put the videos on 1.5x speed to get through them. Overall, a decent class
Best professor by far! She truly cares about her students!

This is probably the best course I've taken as a grad student at UF. The layout of this class is incredible; you essentially get to select a behavior of interest and study it over the course of the class through your 4 assignments. Makes the assignments relevant to your interests and the class presentations fun. Would highly recommend this class!

Professor Emily is a sweet-hearted person. Her online class was the best, however the only thing i didn't like about it was when the written response portion on the quizzes was to in detailed. I wouldn't mind taking her again, especially if you're good with writing papers. You will have 3 quizzes, a research paper, and oral presentation at the end.

This was my second course with Dr. Miller-Cushon. Although this was a much shorter course than the first I took with her (only 1 credit), I still really enjoyed it and learned a lot. There aren't many grad courses at UF that are this straightforward grading-wise and that allow you this much control over the topics for your coursework.

analysis.name: Eamon O'Connor
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Great guy! Had my quest 1 with him, and he was chill. Weekly reading assignments and 1(pretty easy) group project, and 3 essays. Lectures were pretty cool, and I found him engaging and pretty funny. Participation in class encouraged but not necessary if you're the type that worries about that.
One of the best professors I've ever had, very understanding, is easy to understand and has interesting assignments. Would forever take this class again

He's an awesome professor who makes lectures fun. I have a hard time paying attention in general, but he knows how to keep it interesting. Assignments are fun, and interpretation is quite open. It's a really good way for students to really understand and explore their interpretation of an answer, and sharing. Expect to actually enjoy class.

Loved this class! I'd take it again in a heartbeat. It really opened my eyes to the way we tell stories through digital means and even positively influenced my own work. Very caring professor that likes to have fun with his students while teaching, which makes the learning much more enjoyable. Learned about lots of new media as well!

He's great, always fun to go to his class and engages well with the students. He gives some great readings to learn from and the presentations are really funny. I enjoyed a lot of the assignments we did and had fun looking at my classmate's work in critique, was always hilarious and a fun learning environment. Love this guy

analysis.name: George Esenwein
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 43 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: One of the best and foremost history professors at UF. Top notch lectures and his knowledge is beyond bounds for almost all areas of Modern History. I would take everyone one of his courses if I could.

Dr. Esenwein clearly knows his stuff and answers questions well. His exams (usually essays) are manageable if you pay attention to his reviews (which he also posts online) and demonstrate that you know the material. Lectures are straight forward but informative, he is reserved but very helpful and understanding if you go to him. Really solid guy.

He is nice and will help if you ask. Listen to his feedback and ask questions in office hours. Lectures are dry and long. Readings are helpful but not necessary to pass the essay exams. Exams include 2 essays in 50 minutes. One 10 page paper is required. Tough grader, go to writing center for help if you are not confident in your writing.

Solid guy, genuinely interested in the History of European Terrorism. Somewhat boring topic at times, but most should learn a good amount of interesting things about the topic. Very helpful if you come to ask him anything at all. If not, he's very quiet and probably won't talk to you directly (even in the small course setting).

He is a very nice Professor. Very passionate about history and knows European history better than any other teacher I've had at UF. Lectures can get boring, slides are wordy but if you do the readings and/or take really good notes you should do well. No class discussions, just straight lecture and powerpoint. We had two exams & 1 10 page paper.

analysis.name: Feihong Wang
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 73 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Wang was really fun to have and genuinely cared about the class she was teaching. My only issues with the class is that all grading is done by TAs and some of them are very strict with the prompts, and also the exams were open book, but all the exam content was indeed from the book with lectures being very minimal content wise.

The textbook used for this course is super information heavy and hard to take good notes on. The exams & quizzes are open note, but exams are not nearly as easy as the quizzes & the lectures don't really help unless they are directly asked about in the exams. Ctrl F is your friend for this class. Discussion posts & diary entries weren't hard either

She is very sweet and caring, you can tell she cares about her students and wants them to know there is always help from her or TAs. The class wasn't much work, just discussion posts and weekly quizzes. The only thing was the online exams, even though they are open textbook and notes, the questions were very niche/specific. Lock in for the project!

Listen, the class wasn't that difficult, but I literally only scraped by with an A which is insane considering it's psych. Her exams are more of a "find extremely niche information mentioned in one sentence in the textbook" rather than applying relevant knowledge. The lectures were barely relevant. It wasn't horrible, but I wouldn't take it again.

Beware the TAs are very nitpicky on discussion posts. Prof is super nice but her exams are long, tedious, and full of irrelevant questions. Sometimes the answers aren't even in the textbook, which sucks. The material is interesting, but it's an exam-heavy class with literally just one SINGULAR extra-credit opportunity + you actually have to study.

analysis.name: Jack Famiglietti

Very hard to get an A in this class, because this is the most work you will ever do for a 2 credit class. DO NOT put off the project deadlines and be sure to put good effort. He's a pretty good teacher, not a good  test maker. Put things on the test he barely mentioned in lecture. Overall very fun class learned how to use mills, lathes, weld, etc.

Pretty dull instructor, reacted pretty poorly to the class failing the second exam and presented the curve as charity rather than taking personal accountability. New professor but needs much improvement

Failed the entire class on an exam and blamed us for "not studying hard enough". He's new and clearly has no idea what he's doing and though he says he cares about improving his actions suggest otherwise

Dr. Familgietti is an amazing professor. He is always willing to work with the students and give them feedback on their projects. He is VERY clear about what he expects work-wise.

DML is inherently a hard course, but if you show up to lectures, PAY ATTENTION TO THE ANNOUNCEMENTS, and attend office hours on occasion, you will do perfectly fine

analysis.name: Meagan Frenzer
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She makes me want to — myself. There are so many assignments and on each one there are four annotations needed. I can understand this until it's 10-15 assignments each week with four annotations each. And they're graded harshly even when it was clear that effort was made. Would not rec; she's a kind person, but would not want her as a prof again.

2/5. You do learn a lot in this class, but the workload is overwhelming. There are about 7 assignments each week, and each requires 4 annotations. The grading is unclear and inconsistent, with no feedback at all. Some assignments go ungraded for weeks, then many grades are posted at once, making it hard to know how to improve.

Everything about her and her class was lovely. Her lectures were very informative and interesting. She gives extra credit opportunities. She is not a hard grader as long as you follow the prompts, she gives great feedback on assignments and how to improve next time . Would totally take again.

Professor Frenzer's love and excitement for history is reflected in lectures. Her lectures are energized and engaging, but also move at a fast pace. This class is a reading intensive class more than it is a writing intensive class. She grades fairly and justly: the grades she gives are very reasonable. I highly recommend taking Professor Frenzer.

She's a great professor and moves things around when theres an unfortunate event, but as long as you do the journals and show up to class you should be fine. Lectures cover things about the journals but if you dont show up your grade will drop 1/3 of a letter grade which is insane. Also grades come in late and at the end of semester so just beware.

analysis.name: Flora Iff-Noel

She was a great teacher! Her Latin classes are definitely a bit harder than some of the other ones in the department, but they're not bad at all. She seems a little scary sometimes, but she's not, she's super nice!

It was more of a Latin literature class than i was expecting, and WAY more of an augustan-age class than i was expecting, but it was a great class overall! She was knowledgable, and very helpful with answering questions after class.

analysis.name: Alice Freifeld

Course continues to be meaningful to me.

All she does is talk about how smart she is when she can't even figure out how to properly use Canvas. This would be okay, but all she does is brag about how many years of teaching she has. She also never responds to emails.

analysis.name: Faris Kronfli

review_highlights.comment: I would highly recommend this class for psych majors needing to fulfill their requirements. Dr. Kronfli is very easy to reach and willing to help answer any questions. I thought this class was a great experience. The guidelines are very clear and the workload is incredibly reasonable. The workload is not excessive and due bi-weekly. Very easy psychology class. You have to read the textbook to do the assignments and watch the lectures for the quizzes. The material is the same for both, and the quizzes are very straightforward. He's a good professor and makes the class as interesting as possible. The class was easy and enjoyable.

Very accessible through email and willing to explain any concept, question, or concern. I would recommend this class to anyone unless you hate reading a textbook because that is very necessary for this class. Other than that such a good online class and a very helpful professor!

I was nervous about taking this class over Summer A, but it was great! The class was very flexible, only a reading guide, quiz, and discussion post due each week. The quizzes were reflective of the reading guides so I do recommend you watch the lectures and read the textbook. The professor is  accessible and he always answered my questions!

analysis.name: Norman Fitz-Coy
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Rarely if ever uploads any resource material so he can reuse tests over and over. Lecture notes get uploaded sporadically. Lectures are unorganized. Mindless PowerPoint style lectures are unhelpful and written in comic sans. Horrible at explaining. TA just so happened to be unhelpful as well.

Professor Fitz-Coy is up there as one of the worst professors I've had. His explanations are very vague for what he asks of you and you're going to have to teach yourself the material. PPTs are rarely uploaded and are usually late. If you do not go to lecture you'll most likely be completely lost as the book doesn't use the methods he teaches.

Very combative for no reason, just reads off slides and doesn't explain well. Expects grad level expertise out of an undergraduate class. Cool class and concepts. Material was presented very mathematically. Often took a lot of time outside of class to understand the implications of the material. Grade was two exams, both were very fair.

analysis.name: Amy Freeman

review_highlights.comment: she doesnt even have a phd. find a god and pray.

> “Art teacher” teaching a class about business and technology. Need I say more?

Unlicensed, no experience, no degree, doesn't teach, no care for students. She will punish you for medical, family, or personal emergencies rather than accommodating. Not only is she disrespectful to students, but UF staff as well.

She fosters an environment of fear and humiliation. 
There is not a single trace of good in Amy Freeman.
This class was the sole reason i dropped out of the University Of Florida.

analysis.name: Stephanie  Gruver
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

review_highlights.comment: Ms Gruver is very knowledgeable and clearly passionate about the subject. The course is intellectually engaging, but it can be demanding, especially if you're not used to the pace or reading expectations. Lectures are well-structured, though sometimes dense, and participation is important. Grading is fair but requires strong attention to detail.

Incredibly tough grader, rubrics aren't detailed enough for such a high standard that Ms.Gruver seems to have. Inputs her own biases into her grading on subjective topics. I am personally not neurotypical so my answers tend to be more in-depth and nuanced, which is apparently incorrect. She mostly doesn't explain the grading on your assignment.
Terible teacher, thinks her quest class is so important that she is willing to give you a failing grade becasue you missed the lecture one time. would not recommend

Grades a bit harshly, pop quizzes on weekly readings, and very few grades with somewhat vague rubrics. However, readings didn't take too long. As long as you tried on the assignments properly you'd do fine, content was interesting and she understood many were taking her class purely out of a QUEST req. Lots of free points near the end.

Class was not too difficult and not too much work but the main topic of the class is unclear. Professor is nice but a little difficult to reach sometimes, and grades can entirely depend on her mood when grading. READ THE BOOKS. She makes sure you read them, so just read them. Grading criteria can be unclear and not a lot of assignments.

analysis.name: James Gerien-Chen
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

review_highlights.comment: Tests consisted of key terms from lecture/textbook (he gives you a list of them in advance) and analysis of primary/secondary sources that we went over in class. The lectures can be fast and dense, if you get the big picture it's alright. Lot of readings, but they were really interesting! Slow grader, but a good lecturer that wants you to succeed.

Worst class i have ever taken. Does not give clear instructions. NO TECHNOLOGY ALLOWED. Good luck on the quizzes, they are the most obscure terms and questions you'll ever see. Lectures are almost obsolete and does not matter to any of the essays or quizzes. Everything graded is on the textbook or the two books you read for the class.

Lectures didn't really matter to the class at all, but attendance is still mandatory. Quizzes are super difficult and not what he said they'd be like in the beginning of the semester. He's a great guy and very knowledgeable about the subject, he's just not a great teacher.

analysis.name: Asmaa Ghonim
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Asmaa is the best teacher I have ever had.  Not only were the lectures easy to follow, but they were also filled with fun activities such as playing online games.  I 11/10 recommend Asmaa to anyone that plans on taking a literature class at UF.  She is such an inspiration to me that I am going to dress up as her for halloween.  The class is LIT!!!!
She is very sweet. I took her for ENC-1102. She encourages thoughtful exploration of literary and visual art, which I am always a big fan of. I recommend.

Asmar's class is the most fun class I have ever been a part of. Being my first semester here, I didn't know what to expect in a college professor, but right after I met Asmaa i soon became aware of her amazing lecture and teaching skills. Now when i walk through the classroom doors everyday I sit down and await my intellectual journey.

Asmaa is by far my favorite TA I've ever had at UF. She is super relatable to the students. The first day of class we had a discussion relating that week's topic to Harry Potter, and she always brings up interesting topics in class discussions. She's very approachable, & feels like another one of the students, but is also very knowledgeable.

My absolute favorite TA ever. Definitely made my first semester at UF feel a lot more like home and got rid of the "no one cares about you in college" stigma. She genuinely cares, is hilarious, and will sit and guide you through absolutely anything. Definitely take her if you can, for anything!

analysis.name: Jill Goslinga
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 357 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Goslinga, aka as Godzilla in the Busines school, will do everything in her powers to weed you out. Good luck.

You have to work for the grade. The majority of the class gets a C. You need a B for Accounting Majors. Everyone else needs a C to pass. Read all chapters, go to live lecture so you are not distracted watching it online. TA's are very helpful and TZ as well! Quizzes straight forward, and tests are tricky.

Goslinga goes out of her way to make the class hard, but it's not impossible to do well. You really need to practice old problems over and over again. The book wasn't helpful. And definitely go to TZ, they know what they're doing.

The tests are ridiculous and the hw is worth almost nothing considering how long it takes. I got StudyEdge after the first test and still struggled in the class. Even if you understand the material, every single test question is a trick question that requires you to know a very niche piece of information. I wish I got StudyEdge sooner.

You will spend an absurd amount of time for absolutely no points on homework. You will study and actively do practice problems/tests and think you understand the material and then get a 49 on exam 3. It will not matter how much you actually know. Every exam is designed to trick you and I will now have to retake the class at another school.

analysis.name: Lillian Guerra
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Although lectures are dense and fast, they are very important and interesting. Guerra engages students with history that is both obscure and known (it contextualizes realities we are familiar with already). I was hesitant to speak in discussions at first but Guerra made me more confident to. We were graded by participation, 2 essays, and 2 tests.

would not take this class again even if i was paid. multiple students had jobs outside of campus and it was her mission to make life suck, her excuse would be that they could not dedicate enough time to her class and believes her class requires 100% effort over everything, does not care about the students and what they go through. 0 stars 0 effort

Professor Guerra is awesome! She is passionate about what she teaches and I learned so much from her. There is a lot of writing but she doesn't grade to harshly and has given me really good feedback on my writing that has helped me improve. You will have to do the readings and participate in class but if you put in the effort you will get an A.

Dr. Guerra is a very intelligent and passionate professor. She puts a lot of effort into her course and can be a bit intimidating, but if you put in your effort in return you will do fine. Do the readings before each class and take thorough lecture notes. Very rewarding but does take a good deal of outside work

Professor Guerra is absolutely amazing, she provides great lectures and amazing feedback on essays/assignments. Reading is a must, as well as attendance! It was a pretty tough course, but very rewarding.

analysis.name: Molly Gardner
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: Took PHI3930 (Moral Philosophy). Dr. Gardner is a total sweetheart who clearly cares about both the material and her students. Easy essays/quizzes if you do the readings and take good notes. She's not at all a harsh grader by philosophy standards - do not take this or any philosophy course expecting to phone it in and still get an A.

I love Dr. Gardner :) I've taken two classes with her as a STEM major and she makes philosophy approachable and engaging. Her classes are all formatted the same: four quizzes, two papers, and a few extra credit opps. Attendance is mandatory with 5 automatic excused absences. Overall, if you stay on top of things and participate, you'll be fine!

I loved Dr. Gardner!! The content of this class could be very challenging sometimes, but Dr. Gardner explained everything very well. She was super super sweet and genuinely cared about everyone doing well in the class. The homework could be kind of hard sometimes with "trick questions" but shouldn't be hard if you know the material.

Cares about the content and teaches it well, but her quizzes and papers don't reflect the same person she is when she's lecturing. The quizzes seem designed to trip you up and don't truly reflect your knowledge on the topic. I don't recommend taking her - she has unrealistic expectations for an Intro to Philosophy course.

I would heavily advise against taking this course if you want an easy A. She didn't have many assignments, just 3 papers and quizzes, and a final. However, she was an incredibly harsh grader. Extra credit assignments I completed were marked 0 for petty things. Her paper's scored very low averages and she didn't care. Heavy participation needed.

analysis.name: Shu-Jen Huang

review_highlights.comment: Took Mac2311 my freshman fall and got an A. I went to class, did my homework, went to discussion (weekly quizzes), but MOST IMPORTANTLY took every practice exam i could find. Also, study edge was a big help. I put the time in and got an A. Took AP CALC AB in high school and got a 1. You got this! Class has required attendance due to clicker points

I wouldn't mind her testing on terminology & thm stuff if she allowed a cheat sheet on tests. MATLAB is cool to learn, but it can also be a pain. Ec is nice though.

The class also has a terrible TA.

analysis.name: Han XU

Xu Han is an amazing and very understanding professor.She is a great teacher and I learned so much from her. You can tell she really loves her job and she makes me excited to go to class in the mornings. Hopefully I am able to get into her intermediate chinese class next semester. There are a lot of quizzes.

Professor Han Xu or Xu Lao Shi in class was the best Chinese professor I've had and I am taking her again this semester with an earlier time because I wanted her specifically. Really kind and sweet professor! Lots of quizzes though so make sure you study

analysis.name: Quinn Mccoy Hansen

If you are a graduate level student, do NOT take this class. It's frustrating, pure memorization and not educative at all. Doesn't make you think and understand, it makes you memorize useless stuff. Really frustrating.

analysis.name: Daniel O'Neill

Professor O'Neill was an extremely animated and passionate professor who I highly recommend any Poli Sci freshman take Political Theory with. Dr. O'Neill is an amazing lecturer and I highly recommend you take him for an intro level class. His lectures are extremely engaging and although attendance is not mandatory, I recommend going. I don't think its necessary to purchase the textbook to succeed as his lectures pretty much cover all the material you need to know for his exams.

Prof O'neill is such an amazing professor. He clearly loves and understands the topics presented which makes the lectures super engaging. Grades are weekly discussion attendance,  3 exams, and weekly paragraph reading responses. I highly recommend Billsborough as a TA!

He is so smart and loves what he teaches. As long as you attend class, take notes, and study, you'll do well in the class. Three written exams, super straight-forward and easy to ace. Timmy Stolz is the best TA and is always willing to help answer any questions.

analysis.name: Pete  Punthasee

analysis.name: Youngmin Park

I'd highly recommend taking any course with this professor. The exams were very representative of the content covered in class. He also provided ample extra credit opportunities and exam drops. He genuinely cares about his students and wants everyone to succeed.

Dr.Park is by far one of the best teachers i have had! Go to class, he is super nice and lectures well (most times class gets out early). His exams are very representative of what is learned in class and he listens to his students!

analysis.name: Victoria Pagan

She's kind and super understanding about any issues you're having, especially if she can see you're willing to put in effort. Always highly recommend her class -- if she's teaching it, take it.
Dr. Pagan is literally so nice, I love her. She tells the funniest stories and she's very down to earth, but she was literally UF's teacher of the year last year, so she knows her stuff.

dr. pagan is the sweetest. gardens to read and visit is a great quest 1. few assignments and she's an easy grader. she accepts late work ("docks points for it" but i've never gotten anything but an A). she brought us cupcakes for her birthday. she genuinely cares about students and makes an effort to talk to everyone individually. i adore her!!!
This was a super super easy Latin class (I took it back in 2023, forgot to rate her) but she was amazing and so funny. She's also super understanding.

ik you prob think im d riding him after seeing all the bad ratings,but his tests are EXACTLY like the practice guides he gives plus he lets you bring in a cheat sheet.the avg for the final was like a 93 so do w that what u will. yes he might be a boring lecturer but you don't rly need to go to class to learn either way.he's also just a nice guy too

I took this class for my gen ed credit. The class was super easy and pretty interesting. Most of the grading is done by TAs and the quizzes are a little specific at times. Overall pretty easy course. The professor ended up dropping the lowest quiz and discussion post at the end of the semester. The final was a super easy project.

Professor Vasquez was incredibly kind and supportive. As an online student, I appreciated how easy he was to contact. Even if I was getting some points taken off, he made it clear what he wanted to see improvement on. The course was engaging, not overwhelming. Highly recommend this class!
This class is very difficult. Assigned readings are long quizzes are obscure and homework assignments are vague are made harder than necessary.

Took this class over Summer B and was honestly pretty simple and interesting. I'm not a theatre major but the different plays were very interesting and made the assignments enjoyable and easy to get through. Grading was very clear and TA's would respond and grade fairly quickly. Def recommend if you need a humanities/diversity credit or for fun.

analysis.name: Roozbeh Tabrizian
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He is a good lecturer, very lackluster at answering questions. Don't ask him questions. Very enthusiastic teacher.
Bad professor, not someone you want teaching you. He is inaccessible outside of class and treats you terrible.

Great professor! He gives clear and understandable lectures. His example problems actually help you on the homework (and the hw problems are all on chegg). He has 3 exams and no cumulative final. He does a review before each exam and If you study the review and HW problems, you'll ace the exams. Cons: He takes about 2 weeks to grade exams.
One of the best professors I have ever had

analysis.name: Alper Ungor
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 103 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I liked the lectures. The topics are interesting and the professor is enthusiastic about the material. He encourages participation and often asks leading questions to help students feel engaged. You should attend this class, and definitely do the homework. The tests are a bit of a time crunch and you will need to have had the practice.

He spent a ton of time going over the easy concepts and flew through the hard stuff. His lectures are straight from the textbook (examples are even the same) and the discussions do not help at all. I would avoid taking this class with him.

Great teacher, hard exams. he's one of the most professional lecturers I've had, he comes to class prepared. The homework isn't for a grade but you wont pass unless you do it well and understand the concepts.

I heard bad rumors about him prior to taking the course, but let me tell you he is the best professor you could have for this course. The other professors make the class more difficult than it needs to be (it's already difficult enough) and are much more strict on grading. Yes, the material is difficult but he tries to make it understandable

Awesome and extremely professional teacher. His work ethic and class definitely changed the way I view academics; I used to think all that mattered in school was getting an A's but he helped show me value of the material and that has resulted in me having increased interest and dedication to all of future classes and academic endeavors.

analysis.name: Susan Underkoffler
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: It is very clear that she loves what she does. Makes the material fun and I learned a huge amount.

First she gave us assignments on spring break. Guess after complaints she gave us the next week off. Then she moved up the final project date to sooner a week before it was due. Also said that this was due to grades being due on the 26th but had the final exam due on the 28th? Absolutely will not take her again.

Awesome class! She was one of the best professors I ever had, knows this field, and I learned so much in this course! She really cares about students and wants them to do well. Gives lots of time to finish exams and discussion questions and gives extra credit for participation. Not bad to look at either! Too bad she only teaches online.

analysis.name: Suzanne Utley
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Bad. Barely interacted with the class, no actual teaching performed. Read the weekly readings, read the vague modules full of typos, and get punished on vague quizzes because the "prof" refuses to actually teach. Quizzes poorly worded, based on single-sentence minutiae from textbooks. Quality papers given 10% off seemingly arbitrarily. Disgraceful.

The modules do not match the quizzes and the images that are on the quizzes could be numerous things w/out knowing context. Don't let the extra credit fool you -not worth the effort as it was also graded harshly. Sherry took points off for things she said were not in my papers that were in fact IN MY PAPER which tells me she blindly graded my work.

The modules and pictures provided are the only references available for the class. The quizzes are supposed to be based on the modules which is continually incorrect. There are multiple grammatical errors in every single module as well as spelling. Answers to quiz questions are unclear and extremely wordy for the time provided to take the quizzes.

She is by far the worst professor ever. Granted you have to do more work as the class is online, but she did absolutely nothing. She never lectured or taught in anyway. She never engaged with students or added to class discussions. She never replied to emails. Her notes are very basic and never helpful. Quizzes and exams were impossible to pass.

The questions for these quizes are very cryptic and the pictures are very difficult  to see and offer very little context for the question. As a full time parent, student, and employee, I was given very little grace and she has little to no understanding of the challenges we face in day-to-day life. Students with 4.0 GPA's have failed this course.

analysis.name: Anastasia Ulanowicz
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 31 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: She's a great professor who facilitates conversation and lectures well. However, she is a very tough grader and will make you work for an A or B. She's incredibly nice and will help you in office hours. She also offers some extra credit along the way to help you out, although she also gives quite a few pop quizzes and assignments.

Professor Ulanowicz is one of my two favorite professors at UF (The other being Carolyn Kelley)! Her classes are informative (I have been in several) and she makes your writing better, while still maintaining humanity, unlike other professors that I have encountered. She cares about your education and is willing to help you.

Professor Ulanowicz is awesome. She put a lot of background information about the text into the lectures and really encouraged conversations, especially with different perspectives. There could probably do to be a few more assignments but overall super enjoyable class and obviously very smart, engaged professor

Professor Ulanowicz is AMAZING! Take her class if you're genuinely passionate about the subject, and I promise it will be one of the most rewarding educational experiences you can have. Her lectures are information-heavy, and you'll fall behind quickly if you miss class, but she is a phenomenal lecturer. Also a very quick and very fair grader!

One novel per week. With audio books, it wasn't hard. If you can do that, then absolutely take this class. There is nothing about her teaching style that makes anything harder than it should be. There were two 3pg papers and one 8-10pg. Keep up with the reading, don't wait until the last minute to do the papers, and you'll do well and enjoy class.

analysis.name: Larry Ukeiley
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He sucks. People say he's nice but can snap back at you if you ask a question he doesn't like. Makes you do useless derivations that will NEVER appear at a work place. He needs to make this class more practical - final exam covers too much material. It seems that he thinks aerodynamics is the only course his students are taking.

He is huge on doing long meticulous derivations that no one is paying attention to in class and rarely works out actual problems.  This class is made to be difficult, on exams be prepared to apply complicated formulas that you haven't worked out a problem with.  If you have applied a formula before, he likes to ask you to derive it from scratch.

This guy is horrible. All he does is copy the derivations from the book (except he makes tons of mistakes). Because of this, going to class is completely useless; however, it's technically required because he gives unannounced quizzes. Unfortunately you have to take his class if you're an aero student. Just be warned, and be prepared.

This is for EAS4810C. This class is atrocious. Prepare to spend 30+ hours on each lab just to get in the 70-85 range. I'm convinced that Dr. Ukeiley instructs the TAs to grade as hard as possible because there's really no other explanation. Ukeiley's structure for this class will wear down your mental health, so be ready.

Takes getting used to
1st half lots concpts Second half more application
Lots of time to do HW, I would procrastinate, do NOT recomend

Felt unprepared for exams, easier than expected
Past exams useful
introductory true/false to warm you up
short answer to get you going
long question at the end
long Q like HW, easy to predict with HW and past exams

analysis.name: Ulrich Stingl
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: As long as you watch the lectures, you will do fine on the quizzes. Make sure you understand the quiz questions because the exams are very similar to them. Overall, he is very fair and will clearly tell you what you need to know for the tests.

Awesome professor! Study ahead of time, do study guides, watch videos and you will do fine. I learned so much from this class. I would definitely take him again. You will see quiz questions again on the exams.
Dr. Stingl is one of the best professor! He respects his students and gives a lot of help when needed. It is an easy A class as long as you study for it!

Professor Stingl offers fair grading, minimal homework, and well-prepared quizzes for four exams and an optional final. Balanced coursework, respected teaching, and adeptness with online tools define his approach.

Very easy class but still informative! Straight-forward, no BS. Watch the lectures and study a little and you'll be fine. Modules are very short and exams don't cover much material at all. Unless you don't try at all, there's no reason you won't do well in this class. Class could probably be made a little harder but I'm definitely not complaining

analysis.name: Paola Uparela
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: took Quest 1 Comida & Conflicto and even though the class itself is not hard there is a lot of work involved. about 2 quizzes a week about articles that can be more than 10 pgs long and videos that you have to submit on friday's about a particular topic. I honestly thought it was a lot for a quest, but she doesn't have exams so that's good

I took SPN3930 and SPW3030 with Paola and they were my favorite classes I've taken at UF. One of the most knowledgeable/interesting professors I've had. Classes are reading heavy, but she is always excited to help during office hours. Essays have loose prompts, and she encourages thinking creatively & giving our interpretations of the topics/texts.

Interesting lectures but she barely provides a space for students to share their knowledge on the topic. So keep your knowledge to yourself, she knows more than everyone else. The good thing is that you do not need to be creative or original in this class, as long as you write the final paper that she wants you to write, you will get an A.

Fantastic professor. There's a lot of reading in this class, but that's to be expected, it is a literature class. She was always very available outside of class, and really wants her students to be successful. Great feedback on all assignments.
